A Wedding Destination Guide

भारतदर्शन BharatDarshan

Discover the soul of India

The Bhat Family and Alexandre Rossignol warmly welcome you to India. We are delighted to share our culture and roots with you and look forward to celebrating our wedding together. This website will help guide your itinerary if you wish to explore India beyond the wedding.

Scroll
Destinations

Travel Recommendations यात्रा

Extraordinary destinations across India

Important Information 🍷: Gujarat is a dry state, where the sale, consumption, and possession of alcohol 🍺 are PROHIBITED BY LAW. The policy reflects the values of Mahatma Gandhi, who was born in Gujarat and strongly supported prohibition. Travelers are advised to respect local regulations during their visit.
✦
💡 Tip: Click markers to explore travel highlights across India, organized into three main categories: 🛕 Temple Architecture & Heritage, 🏖️ Beaches & Islands, ⛰️ Hill Stations, and 🌆 Big City Life.
Travel Recommendations
🚆 Important Travel Tip: India's trains and popular hotels book out weeks or even months in advance, especially during the November–December festive and wedding season. We strongly recommend reserving your accommodation and train tickets as early as possible to secure the best options and prices.
ℹ️ Please note: Recommendations for Ahmedabad are personal suggestions from the Bhat family. Recommendations for other cities may draw on external sources including Google. These are guidelines only — please conduct your own research and do not rely solely on these suggestions. India is wonderfully diverse and there is much more to discover!

Ahmedabad — अहमदाबाद

❤️ Home State ❤️

Gujarat's vibrant cultural capital, a UNESCO World Heritage City with over 600 years of history, extraordinary step-wells, and the warmth of home.

Adalaj ni Vav

A stunning 15th-century step-well adorned with intricate carvings — one of the finest examples of Indo-Islamic architecture. Magical in morning light.

Gandhi Ashram

The historic Sabarmati Ashram on the banks of the river — Mahatma Gandhi's home and the spiritual birthplace of India's independence movement.

Sidi Saiyyed Mosque

The Sidi Saiyyed Mosque is renowned for its exquisite stone lattice work, especially the iconic “Tree of Life” carving. Built in the 16th century, it represents the finest example of Indo-Islamic architecture in Gujarat and remains a symbol of Ahmedabad’s rich artistic heritage.

Gujarat — गुजरात

Gujarat is known for its rich cultural heritage, historic architecture, and vibrant traditions. The state offers a mix of ancient craftsmanship, colorful festivals, traditional handicrafts, and diverse landscapes ranging from deserts to coastal regions.

Akshardham Temple

A monumental Hindu temple complex in Gandhinagar, renowned for its pink sandstone architecture, spiritual ambiance and beautifully landscaped gardens.

Sun Temple, Modhera

An exquisite 11th-century temple dedicated to the sun god Surya, known for its celestial design that aligns perfectly with the equinoxes.

Statue of Unity

The world's tallest statue at 182 meters, a tribute to Sardar Vallabhbhai Patel, set amidst the dramatic Narmada valley. A full-day excursion.

Nal Sarovar

A vast freshwater wetland sanctuary, especially magical in winter when flamingos and migratory birds arrive in stunning flocks.

Laxmi Vilas Palace

A breathtaking Indo-Saracenic royal palace in Vadodara (2-3 hours by bus from Ahmedabad), four times the size of Buckingham Palace and still a royal residence.

Rann of Kutch — कच्छ का रण

The world's largest salt desert, ethereal under the full moon, alive with folk music, crafts, and the legendary Rann Utsav festival (Nov–Feb).

Great Rann White Desert

The iconic salt flats that stretch to the horizon — especially stunning under the full moon. The Rann Utsav festival runs November through February.

Wild Ass Sanctuary

Home to the endangered Indian wild ass, flamingos, and wolves. Winter is the ideal season for wildlife spotting in this vast, unique ecosystem.

Bhuj Old Town & Crafts

The cultural hub of Kutch, famed for embroidery, Rogan art, and traditional weaving. Visit the Aina Mahal and Prag Mahal palaces.

Rann Utsav Festival

The Rann Utsav (November–February) is a government-organized cultural festival set on the edge of the White Rann. Visitors can book all-inclusive packages through the official rannutsav.in website — the Tent City offers comfortable tents with attached washrooms, cultural performances (folk music, dance, fire acts), camel and horse rides, ATV adventures, craft bazaars, and authentic Gujarati cuisine under a sky full of stars. Packages range from 1 to several nights. It is one of India's most magical seasonal festivals and an absolute highlight of any winter visit.

Udaipur — उदयपुर

The "City of Lakes," Rajasthan's most romantic city with marble palaces rising from shimmering waters. Ideal in November–December with pleasant temperatures.

City Palace

A magnificent 400-year-old palace complex on Lake Pichola, blending Mughal and Rajput architecture. The largest palace complex in Rajasthan.

Lake Palace (Taj Hotel)

The legendary white marble palace floating on Lake Pichola — even just an evening dinner here is an unforgettable experience.

Jag Mandir Island

A beautiful palace on an island in Lake Pichola, accessible by boat. The sunset views over the lake and city are breathtaking.

Saheliyon Ki Bari

The "Garden of the Maidens," an 18th-century royal garden with fountains, marble pavilions, and lotus pools — a serene retreat.

Jaipur — जयपुर

The "Pink City," Rajasthan's capital, dazzling with amber fortresses, bazaars overflowing with gems and textiles, and the iconic Hawa Mahal.

Amber Fort

A majestic hill fort of amber-colored stone with stunning mirror rooms (Sheesh Mahal), elephant rides, and panoramic views over the valley below.

Hawa Mahal

The iconic "Palace of Winds" — a five-story sandstone screen with 953 small windows, built so royal ladies could watch street life while remaining unseen.

Jantar Mantar (UNESCO)

An extraordinary 18th-century astronomical observatory with massive stone instruments still accurate to this day. A UNESCO World Heritage Site.

City Palace & Bazaars

The royal City Palace complex and the legendary bazaars for gems, block-print textiles, blue pottery, and silver jewelry. Shopping heaven.

Rajasthan — राजस्थान

Rajasthan is known for its grand forts, palaces, and vibrant royal history. The state offers a rich blend of desert landscapes, colorful culture, traditional arts, and lively festivals.

Mount Abu Dilwara Jain Temples

The Dilwara Temples are among the finest examples of Jain temple architecture in India. Built between the 11th and 13th centuries, they are famous for their stunning white marble work, with incredibly intricate carvings on ceilings, pillars, and doorways.

Agra — आगरा

Agra, located in Uttar Pradesh, is home to the iconic Taj Mahal and rich Mughal heritage. Cool, clear weather in November–December makes it perfect for sightseeing and photography.

Taj Mahal (UNESCO)

The world's most celebrated monument to love — visit at sunrise for the most ethereal light and fewer crowds. A non-negotiable on any India journey.

Agra Fort (UNESCO)

A massive Mughal fort-palace complex where Shah Jahan spent his final years as a prisoner, gazing at the Taj Mahal across the river.

Mehtab Bagh

The "Moonlit Garden" directly across the Yamuna from the Taj Mahal — the perfect spot for sunset reflections and stunning photographs.

Fatehpur Sikri (UNESCO)

A perfectly preserved 16th-century Mughal ghost city, 40km from Agra — Akbar's capital, abandoned due to a water shortage. Hauntingly beautiful.

Darjeeling — दार्जिलिंग

The "Queen of the Hills" — misty tea gardens, panoramic Himalayan views including Kangchenjunga, and a charming colonial hill town atmosphere.

Tea Gardens & Tea Tasting

Walk through the emerald terraces of Happy Valley or Makaibari tea estates. Guided tours include a tasting of the world's finest "champagne of teas."

Tiger Hill Sunrise

Wake before dawn for the legendary Tiger Hill sunrise — on clear days, you'll see golden light flood Kangchenjunga (8,586m) and even distant Everest.

Toy Train (UNESCO)

The Darjeeling Himalayan Railway — a UNESCO World Heritage steam train that winds through tea gardens and mountain villages. A timeless experience.

Batasia Loop & Mall Road

The scenic loop with Himalayan panoramas, and the charming Mall Road lined with Tibetan crafts, momos, and incredible mountain views.

Andaman & Nicobar Islands — अंडमान और निकोबार द्वीप समूह

India's tropical paradise — pristine white sand beaches, luminescent bioluminescent bays, world-class snorkeling and scuba diving. November–February is the very best time to visit.

Radhanagar Beach (Havelock)

Consistently ranked among Asia's finest beaches — turquoise waters, dramatic sunsets, and a serene jungle-backed shoreline. Stay overnight on Havelock island.

Cellular Jail, Port Blair

The historic "Kala Pani" prison where freedom fighters were exiled by the British. The evening light-and-sound show is deeply moving.

Scuba Diving & Snorkeling

The coral reefs at Elephant Beach and Neil Island are among India's most spectacular. November–April offers crystal-clear visibility — perfect for beginners too.

Bioluminescent Beaches

On moonless nights, the shoreline glows electric blue from bioluminescent plankton — one of nature's most extraordinary and magical spectacles.

Tamil Nadu — तमिल नाडु

A land of grand Dravidian temples, sacred traditions, and timeless cultural heritage.

Thanjavur

Home to the magnificent Brihadeeswarar Temple—a Chola-era architectural marvel carved in stone, radiating symmetry, power, and devotion.

Madurai

A vibrant temple city centered around the iconic Meenakshi Amman Temple, where colorful gopurams narrate mythological stories.

Srirangam

An island town housing the sprawling Ranganathaswamy Temple—one of the largest functioning temple complexes in the world.

Kumbakonam

Known for its cluster of ancient temples and sacred tanks, often called the “Temple Town of South India.”

Mahabalipuram

A coastal heritage site famed for rock-cut monuments like the Shore Temple and intricate stone carvings.

Karnataka — कर्नाटक

❤️ Home State ❤️

A land where intricate stone architecture meets lost empires and lush landscapes.

Belur & Halebeedu

Twin temple towns showcasing exquisite Hoysala craftsmanship—intricate carvings that seem almost alive.

Hampi

The hauntingly beautiful ruins of the Vijayanagara Empire—boulder-strewn landscapes dotted with temples and royal remnants.

Gol Gumbaz

A massive mausoleum famous for its whispering gallery and impressive dome architecture.

Badami

Ancient cave temples carved into sandstone cliffs—an early masterpiece of Indian rock-cut architecture.

Madhya Pradesh — मध्य प्रदेश

The heart of India, rich with ancient temples, intricate sculptures, and deep history.

Khajuraho

A UNESCO World Heritage site known for its intricately sculpted temples celebrating life, spirituality, and art.

Odisha — ओडिशा

A coastal state famed for its majestic temples and intricate stone carvings.

Konark Sun Temple

A colossal chariot-shaped temple dedicated to the sun god—renowned for its intricate stone wheels and sculptures.

Goa — गोवा

Small, scenic state on India’s west coast, renowned for its 100+ km coastline, stunning beaches, and vibrant nightlife. Former portuguese colony until 1961.

Goa

Golden beaches, Portuguese heritage, vibrant nightlife, and a laid-back coastal vibe.

Shimla — शिमला

A popular hill station with colonial charm, pleasant weather, and bustling promenades.

The Ridge

Open space with panoramic views and colonial architecture.

Mall Road

The main street lined with shops, cafés, and heritage buildings.

Jakhoo Temple

A hilltop temple offering scenic views of the town.

Kullu Manali — कुल्लू मनाली

Famous for snow-capped mountains, adventure activities, and picturesque valleys.

Solang Valley

Popular for adventure sports and snow activities.

Rohtang Pass

A high mountain pass known for dramatic landscapes and snow.

Hadimba Temple

An ancient temple set amidst cedar forests.

Dharamshala — धर्मशाला

Known for its Tibetan culture, monasteries, and serene mountain surroundings.

McLeod Ganj

A vibrant area known for Tibetan culture and cafés.

Namgyal Monastery

A peaceful monastery associated with the Dalai Lama.

Bhagsu Waterfall

A scenic waterfall popular for short hikes.

Kashmir — कश्मीर

Renowned for breathtaking landscapes, lakes, and valleys, often called “Paradise on Earth.”

Dal Lake

Famous for houseboats and shikara rides.

Gulmarg

A scenic hill destination known for meadows and skiing.

Pahalgam

A picturesque valley with rivers, greenery, and trekking routes.

Gangtok — गंगटोक

A clean and scenic hill town offering mountain views, monasteries, and a peaceful blend of nature and culture.

Tsomgo Lake

A high-altitude glacial lake surrounded by mountains.

Rumtek Monastery

One of the most important monasteries in Sikkim with peaceful surroundings.

MG Marg

A lively, clean promenade ideal for walks, shopping, and local food.

Ooty — ऊटी

A charming hill station known for tea gardens, rolling hills, and a mild climate.

Ooty Lake

A scenic lake ideal for boating and relaxation.

Botanical Gardens

Well-maintained gardens with diverse plant species.

Doddabetta Peak

The highest point in the Nilgiris offering wide views.

Madikeri — मडिकेरी

A tranquil destination in Coorg, famous for coffee plantations, misty hills, and lush greenery.

Abbey Falls

A beautiful waterfall surrounded by coffee plantations.

Raja’s Seat

A viewpoint known for stunning sunrise and sunset views.

Madikeri Fort

A historic fort reflecting the region’s past.

Mount Abu — माउंट आबू

The only hill station in Rajasthan, offering a cool escape with lakes, greenery, and scenic viewpoints.

Nakki Lake

A peaceful lake surrounded by hills, ideal for boating.

Sunset Point

A popular spot for panoramic sunset views.

Mumbai — मुंबई

India’s financial capital, known for its fast-paced life, Bollywood, and coastal charm.

Gateway of India

An iconic waterfront monument and historic landmark.

Marine Drive

A scenic seaside promenade, especially beautiful at sunset.

Colaba Causeway

A lively street for shopping, cafés, and local culture.

Bengaluru — बेंगलुरु

A modern tech hub blending pleasant weather with parks, cafés, and a vibrant startup culture.

Lalbagh Botanical Garden

A historic garden with diverse plant species and a glasshouse.

Cubbon Park

A green oasis in the heart of the city, ideal for relaxing walks.

Bangalore Palace

A grand structure inspired by European architecture.

Delhi — दिल्ली

A city of contrasts, combining ancient history with modern urban life and diverse cuisine.

Red Fort

A UNESCO-listed Mughal fort showcasing India’s history.

India Gate

A war memorial and popular gathering spot.

Qutub Minar

A towering minaret and architectural marvel from the Delhi Sultanate era.

Kolkata — कोलकाता

Known for its intellectual heritage, colonial architecture, and rich cultural traditions.

Howrah Bridge

An iconic cantilever bridge and symbol of the city.

Victoria Memorial

A grand marble monument reflecting colonial history.

Dakshineswar Kali Temple

A revered riverside temple with spiritual significance.

Chennai — चेन्नई

A coastal city known for its classical culture, temples, and South Indian traditions.

Marina Beach

One of the longest urban beaches, lively and scenic.

Kapaleeshwarar Temple

A vibrant Dravidian-style temple with intricate carvings.

Fort St. George

A historic British-era fort marking the city’s colonial past.

Wedding Planning

Wedding Schedule कार्यक्रम

A simple overview of all events and timings

✧

The full schedule will be shared closer to the date. In the meantime, next section is a general overview of the traditional events that make up an Indian wedding — each one unique, joyful, and an opportunity to dress in the vibrant colors of India.

Event Date Time Location
Mehendi -- -- --
Haldi -- -- --
Sangeet -- -- --
Wedding Ceremony -- -- --
Benediction (TBD) -- -- --
Celebrations

Wedding Clothing Guide विवाह

A guide to dressing beautifully across each ceremony.

✦
🌸 These are only guidelines. Indian weddings warmly encourage colorful, creative, and personal expression. There are no strict rules — the more color and joy you bring, the better! Feel free to interpret these suggestions in your own beautiful way.

👗 How many outfits do I need? The celebrations take place over two days (the 25th and 26th). As a minimum, one outfit per day works perfectly well — simply choose something versatile and festive that suits the spirit of both events on that day. If you'd like to go all out, you're very welcome to wear a different outfit for each individual ceremony, which would mean up to four outfits in total. Entirely up to you — both approaches are wonderful!
Pooja

About the Ceremony

A sacred Hindu prayer ceremony that marks the beginning of the wedding celebrations. The family gathers to seek blessings from the divine for the union ahead.

What to Expect

Intimate and spiritual atmosphere with traditional rituals, chanting, flowers, incense, and offerings around a sacred fire (havan kund). A deeply meaningful start to the festivities.

Suggested Attire

👔 Male
👗 Female

Suggested Colors

Ivory Soft Green Pale Yellow Blush Pink Lilac

Avoid black and white (reserved for mourning)

Haldi

About the Ceremony

The haldi (turmeric) ceremony is a joyful, playful ritual where the couple is anointed with a paste of turmeric, sandalwood, and rose water to bless and beautify their skin before the wedding.

What to Expect

It gets messy — and that's the point! Family members lovingly smear haldi on the bride and groom. Expect laughter, music, and a cloud of golden yellow. Wear something you don't mind staining!

Suggested Attire

👔 Male
👗 Female

Suggested Colors

Bright Yellow Mustard Saffron

Yellow, orange, and turmeric tones are traditional and most welcome!

Mehendi & Sangeet

About the Ceremony

Mehendi: The bride's hands and feet are adorned with intricate henna designs — a centuries-old art symbolising love, prosperity, and the beauty of new beginnings.

Sangeet: An evening of music, dance, and celebration where both families come together to perform and revel in song.

What to Expect

The mehendi ceremony is intimate and artistic. The sangeet is the most festive pre-wedding event — think Bollywood dances, live music, and pure joy! Feel free to join in!

Suggested Attire

👔 Male
👗 Female

Suggested Colors

Deep Pink Purple Royal Blue Emerald Orange

Bold, vibrant colors are celebrated. This is the night to shine!

Lagan — The Wedding

About the Ceremony

The Lagan is the main wedding ceremony — a sacred Hindu ritual conducted around the sacred fire (Agni). The bride and groom take their vows (saptapadi — seven steps) before the fire as the divine witness.

What to Expect

A grand, deeply emotional, and spiritually beautiful ceremony. Expect elaborate rituals, the exchange of garlands (varmala), and the sindoor ceremony.

Suggested Attire

👔 Male
👗 Female

Suggested Colors

Deep Red Magenta Deep Maroon Navy Gold

Rich, regal tones. Please avoid white (worn by widows in Hindu tradition) and black.

🕊️ Bénédiction (TBD)

About the Ceremony

A ceremony where a priest blesses the couple. It includes a reading from the Bible, a prayer, and a blessing over the couple asking for love, strength, and guidance in their life together.

What to Expect

It is a quiet and respectful moment focused on support, unity, and good wishes for the future.

Suggested Attire

👔
Male

Suit or smart blazer with trousers. A tie is optional but adds a polished touch.

👗
Female

Cocktail dress, midi dress, or elegant skirt and blouse. Soft florals and pastels work beautifully.

Suggested Colors

Champagne Ivory Sage Green Powder Blue Dusty Rose Charcoal Warm Taupe

Soft, elegant tones suit the intimate nature of this occasion.

For Our Guests

Travel Tips सुझाव

Tailored advice for French and German travellers

✦

🇫🇷 Pour les Voyageurs Français

  • Visa: Les ressortissants français doivent obtenir un visa électronique (eTV) pour se rendre en Inde. Il convient d'en faire la demande en ligne sur indianvisaonline.gov.in au moins 4 jours avant le départ (jusqu'à 30 jours à l'avance). Les frais s'élèvent à environ 25 €, et le traitement est généralement rapide, mais il est recommandé de faire la demande suffisamment à l'avance.
  • Vols: Air France propose des vols directs entre Paris CDG et Mumbai ou Delhi. Les correspondances via Dubaï, Abou Dhabi ou Doha sont souvent plus abordables. Ahmedabad (AMD) bénéficie de bonnes correspondances via Mumbai.
  • Langue: L'anglais est largement parlé dans les zones touristiques et les hôtels. Quelques mots d'hindi (Namaste, Dhanyavaad = merci) sont très appréciés. Le français est rarement parlé.
  • Santé: Consultez votre médecin pour connaître les vaccins recommandés (hépatite A, fièvre typhoïde). Il est vivement recommandé de souscrire une assurance voyage incluant une couverture médicale. Emportez avec vous tous les médicaments qui vous ont été prescrits.
  • Nourriture: Le Gujarat est une région majoritairement végétarienne — une merveilleuse découverte pour beaucoup. Si vous aimez les plats épicés, laissez-vous tenter ! La cuisine indienne est d'une diversité extraordinaire, bien au-delà de ce que l'on connaît en France.
  • Eau: ne buvez que de l'eau en bouteille. Dans les bons hôtels et restaurants, l'hygiène alimentaire est la norme, mais soyez prudent au début avec la nourriture vendue dans la rue.
  • Météo: Novembre-décembre est la période idéale, avec des températures agréables comprises entre 18 et 28 °C à Ahmedabad. Les soirées peuvent être fraîches, une veste légère est utile.
  • Prises électriques: En Inde, les prises électriques sont de types C, D et M, fonctionnant avec une tension de 230 V et une fréquence de 50 Hz. Les fiches européennes (type C) fonctionnent parfois, mais un adaptateur vers le type D ou M est généralement nécessaire. Les batteries externes sont très utiles.
  • Monnaie: Roupie indienne (INR). On trouve des distributeurs automatiques de billets un peu partout. Prévenez votre banque avant votre départ. Les paiements via UPI sont courants, mais nécessitent un numéro de téléphone indien — prévoyez un peu d'argent liquide.
  • Sécurité: L'Inde est généralement un pays très sûr pour les touristes. Faites preuve d'une vigilance normale, gardez des copies de vos documents et utilisez des moyens de transport fiables. La famille est toujours là pour vous aider !
  • Trains: Réservez sur le site web de l'IRCTC ou via Cleartrip/MakeMyTrip. Les trains express Rajdhani et Shatabdi sont confortables et offrent une expérience typiquement indienne.

🇩🇪 Für Deutsche Reisende

  • Visum: Deutsche Staatsbürger benötigen für eine Einreise nach Indien ein E-Visum (eTV). Beantragen Sie dieses mindestens 4 Tage vor Reiseantritt (bis zu 30 Tage im Voraus) online unter indianvisaonline.gov.in . Die Gebühr beträgt etwa 25 €, und die Bearbeitung erfolgt in der Regel zügig, dennoch wird eine frühzeitige Beantragung empfohlen.
  • Flüge: Lufthansa fliegt direkt von Frankfurt und München nach Mumbai, Delhi und Ahmedabad. Verbindungen über Dubai oder Doha sind oft günstiger.
  • Sprache: Englisch wird in Touristengebieten und Hotels weitgehend gesprochen. Ein paar Hindi-Worte (Namaste, Dhanyavaad = Danke) werden sehr geschätzt.
  • Gesundheit: Impfberatung beim Hausarzt (Hepatitis A, Typhus empfohlen). Reisekrankenversicherung mit Rücktransport dringend empfohlen. Eigene Medikamente mitbringen.
  • Essen: Gujarat ist eine überwiegend vegetarische Region – für viele eine wunderbare Entdeckung. Wenn Sie scharfes Essen mögen, lassen Sie sich verwöhnen! Die indische Küche ist außerordentlich vielfältig und geht weit über das hinaus, was man in Deutschland kennt.
  • Trinkwasser: Nur Flaschenwasser trinken. In guten Hotels und Restaurants ist hygienisches Essen Standard, aber bei Straßenessen anfangs vorsichtig sein.
  • Wetter: November–Dezember ist ideal — angenehme 18–28°C in Ahmedabad. Evenings can be cool, a light jacket is useful. In Darjeeling significantly colder (5–15°C).
  • Pünktlichkeit: "Indian Standard Time" — Veranstaltungen beginnen oft mit Verzögerung. Entspannen und genießen ist die beste Strategie!
  • Strom: In Indien gibt es Steckdosen der Typen C, D und M, die mit einer Spannung von 230 V und einer Frequenz von 50 Hz betrieben werden. Europäische Stecker (Typ C) funktionieren manchmal, aber in der Regel ist ein Adapter auf Typ D oder M erforderlich. Externe Akkus sind sehr nützlich.
  • Währung: Indische Rupie (INR). Geldautomaten sind fast überall zu finden. Informieren Sie Ihre Bank vor Ihrer Abreise. Zahlungen über UPI sind üblich, erfordern jedoch eine indische Telefonnummer – nehmen Sie etwas Bargeld mit.
  • Sicherheit: Indien ist für Touristen im Allgemeinen ein sehr sicheres Land. Seien Sie wie immer vorsichtig, bewahren Sie Kopien Ihrer Dokumente auf und nutzen Sie zuverlässige Verkehrsmittel. Die Familie ist immer für Sie da, um Ihnen zu helfen!
  • Züge: Buchen Sie auf der Website der IRCTC oder über Cleartrip/MakeMyTrip. Die Expresszüge Rajdhani und Shatabdi sind komfortabel und bieten ein typisch indisches Reiseerlebnis.

"The Bhat Family is very happy to welcome you to their home and land and looks forward to celebrating together."

BharatDarshan — भारतदर्शन  ·  A Wedding Destination Guide
